name: CARTERS INC
business: Carter's, Inc. is North America's largest apparel company exclusively for babies and young children, selling apparel and accessories under brands including Carter's, OshKosh B'gosh, Skip Hop, Little Planet, and Otter Avenue. The company sells to consumers through company-operated retail stores, eCommerce websites, mobile app, and wholesale distribution channels, as well as through licensees.
sellsTo: Wholesale customers (major retailers such as Walmart, Target, Amazon, Costco, JCPenney, Kohl's, Macy's, Marshalls, Ross Stores, Sam's Club, T.J. Maxx)

exposures.sentence: Our core brands are Carter's and OshKosh B'gosh (or OshKosh), iconic and among the sector's most trusted names.

Our U.S. Retail segment includes sales of our products through our U.S. retail stores and eCommerce sites, including through our omni-channel capabilities which provide our consumers with a seamless shopping experience in the channel of their choice.

catalysts.event: Closure of approximately 60 low-margin stores in fiscal 2026
catalysts.sentence: We closed 35 stores across fiscal 2025 and expect to close approximately 60 stores across fiscal 2026.

catalysts.event: Completion of organizational restructuring reducing offices-based roles by approximately 15%
catalysts.sentence: The plan is expected to reduce offices-based roles by approximately 15% by the end of the second quarter of fiscal 2026 and to generate approximately $35 million in annual savings beginning in fiscal 2026.

tamClaims.claim: The baby and young children's apparel market in the United States is approximately $29 billion as of December 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: The baby and young children's apparel market, ages zero to 10, in the United States is approximately $29 billion as of December 2025.
